name: Plumbing-Geek
description: A website for Brian's Plumbing Works (doing business as Plumbing-Geek), a Portland, Oregon plumbing contractor and vintage plumbing restoration business. The existing site is an outdated mid-2000s SiteBuildIt-era website with plain HTTP, fixed-width non-responsive layout, old sidebar text-link navigation, legacy PageSpeed image URLs, broken HTML rendering, and dated PayPal integrations. The business is active, with forty years of experience as of December 2024, Oregon CCB License #46846, phone number 503-656-6192, and content referencing recent projects.
personaStyleGuide: Build the site as a tactile, editorial tribute to expert craft: industrial precision meets vintage restoration. Use typography as the primary visual anchor, with oversized condensed headlines, measured serif accents, and generous negative space instead of boxed UI. The composition should feel cardless and architectural, with full-bleed imagery of pipes, fittings, restored fixtures, workshop textures, and in-progress restoration details. Favor asymmetric editorial splits, strong horizontal rules, oversized section labels, and bold scale contrast between headings and body copy. Color should draw from aged brass, oxidized metal, porcelain white, and workshop charcoal, creating a premium restoration mood rather than generic contractor blue. Shadows should be minimal and dry, used only to separate layers subtly; rely more on contrast, texture, and spacing than elevation. Spacing should feel airy and deliberate, with large vertical rhythm and sharp alignment. The overall vibe is seasoned, local, and exacting: a master tradesman with a collector's eye for historic plumbing.
features: Responsive redesign replacing the fixed-width non-responsive layout
Clear presentation of Portland, Oregon plumbing contractor and vintage plumbing restoration services
Prominent display of forty years of experience as of December 2024
Direct contact information with phone number 503-656-6192 and Oregon CCB License #46846

sections.title: Hero
sections.description: Introduce Brian's Plumbing Works, doing business as Plumbing-Geek, as a Portland, Oregon plumbing contractor and vintage plumbing restoration business. The hero should immediately surface the business name, the Portland, Oregon location, the dual focus on plumbing contractor work and vintage plumbing restoration, and the phone number 503-656-6192.
sections.layoutHint: Full-bleed hero with a dramatic edge-to-edge restoration or workshop image, massive typographic headline stacked asymmetrically, and a cardless contact callout anchored directly into the composition.

sections.title: Experience & License
sections.description: Highlight forty years of experience as of December 2024 and Oregon CCB License #46846 as the core trust signals.
sections.layoutHint: Use oversized numeric typography for the experience statement with a restrained side column for Oregon CCB License #46846, separated by bold rules rather than cards.

sections.title: Services
sections.description: Present the business as both a plumbing contractor and a vintage plumbing restoration business, keeping the language tightly aligned to those exact service areas.
sections.layoutHint: Two-column editorial layout with one side dedicated to plumbing contractor work and the other to vintage plumbing restoration, using open space, image bleed, and sectional typography instead of boxed service tiles.

sections.title: Recent Projects
sections.description: Create a section for content referencing recent projects, reinforcing that the business is active and currently working on notable plumbing and restoration jobs.
sections.layoutHint: Masonry-style image and text composition with staggered project blocks, cropped detail photography, and caption-driven storytelling without conventional card chrome.

sections.title: Contact
sections.description: Provide a clean contact endpoint centered on the phone number 503-656-6192, with the business identity and Oregon CCB License #46846 repeated for clarity.
sections.layoutHint: Large-format closing panel with bold phone typography, generous whitespace, and a grounded workshop-material backdrop rather than a standard boxed contact form.

branding.aesthetic: Industrial heritage refined into a premium editorial web presence, mixing restoration workshop grit with polished composition and museum-like restraint.

imagery.style: High-contrast documentary photography with tactile close-ups of vintage fixtures, brass fittings, porcelain surfaces, pipework, and active restoration details in natural workshop light.
